---
title: "Tutorials"
subtitle: "Open-source R tutorials for quantitative methods"
description: "Original R tutorials by Alex Newhouse for undergraduate quantitative methods courses, used in CU Boulder's PSCI 2075."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
---

A series of original tutorials I developed for CU Boulder's PSCI 2075 (Quantitative Methods). They're designed for students with **no prior coding experience**, with a focus on building intuition before syntax. Free to use, share, and remix.

## The series

::: {.an-grid}

::: {.an-card}
### [1. Greek Notation & Descriptive Stats](tutorial1.html)
Build the bridge from formal notation (Σ, μ, σ) to R code. Means, variance, and standard deviation from first principles.
[`R` `Intro`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [2. The Pipe Operator](tutorial2-pipe.html)
The single most important readability trick in modern R. Why `%>%` and `|>` exist and when to use each.
[`R` `tidyverse`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [3. Group, Filter, Mutate, Plot](tutorial3.html)
The everyday verbs of data manipulation in `dplyr`, with `ggplot2` to visualize the results.
[`R` `dplyr` `ggplot2`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [4. Variable Transformations](tutorial4.html)
Distributions, log transforms, and recoding — when to reach for each, and what they do to your inferences.
[`R` `Stats`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [5. Using AI Chatbots to Learn R](tutorial5.html)
Strategies for productive LLM-assisted coding without short-circuiting your own learning.
[`R` `LLMs` `Pedagogy`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [6. File Systems & Working Directories](tutorial6.html)
The single biggest source of "it works on my machine" pain. Paths, projects, and `here::here()`.
[`R` `RStudio`]{.tag}

[Open tutorial →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

:::

## Sample syllabi

- [INTD 1027 — The Study of Post-War Fascism](intd1027-syllabus.html) (Middlebury College)
- [PSCI 2075 — Study Guide](psci_2075_study_guide.html) (CU Boulder)

## Feedback

Spotted an error? Have a suggestion? [Email me](mailto:alex.newhouse@colorado.edu) — I update these every term.
